originalTitle: Tim Cook says RAM expenses are ‘unsustainable’ and Apple is going to raise prices
summary: Apple is planning to raise prices in response to the ongoing memory shortage. In an interview with The Wall Street Journal, Apple CEO Tim Cook says "price increases are unavoidable:" We're doing our best to mitigate the huge increases that are being passed to us, and we've been trying to shield our customers from the […]
content: Apple is planning to raise prices in response to the ongoing memory shortage. In an interview with The Wall Street Journal, Apple CEO Tim Cook says “price increases are unavoidable:” >  We’re doing our best to mitigate the huge increases that are being passed to us, and we’ve been trying to shield our customers from the increases, but the situation has become unsustainable. Cook doesn’t say when Apple plans on raising prices or which products will be affected. The company has already stopped selling the Mac Studio with 512GB of RAM in March and later raised the starting price of the Mac Mini to $799 after dropping the cheaper $599 option from its lineup. Analyst Tim Culpan also suggested that Apple could discontinue the base configuration of the MacBook Neo, while keeping the $699 model with 512GB of storage. As AI companies continue to demand more memory in their sprawling data centers, suppliers are struggling to keep up. The shortage has led to surging RAM and storage costs, as well as price increases across game consoles, laptops, and other devices. “There’s less supply at a time when consumers want devices and the memory guys are passing along huge price increases,” Cook tells the WSJ. “We definitely need memory pricing and supply to return to reasonable levels for consumer products.” Apple is getting ready to take the wraps off its latest lineup of iPhones later this year, though it’s unclear how big an impact the memory shortage will have on pricing. The WSJ estimates that the upcoming iPhone 18 Pro could cost $1,299, a jump from the $1,099 iPhone 17 Pro.
chineseTitle: 蒂姆·库克表示 RAM 支出“不可持续”，苹果将提高价格
chineseSummary: 苹果计划提高价格以应对持续的内存短缺。苹果首席执行官蒂姆·库克在接受《华尔街日报》采访时表示，“价格上涨是不可避免的：”我们正在尽最大努力减轻正在传递给我们的大幅上涨，并且我们一直在努力保护我们的客户免受[...]
chineseContent: 苹果计划提高价格以应对持续的内存短缺。苹果首席执行官蒂姆·库克在接受《华尔街日报》采访时表示，“价格上涨是不可避免的”：>我们正在尽最大努力减轻转嫁到我们身上的巨额涨价，并且我们一直在努力保护我们的客户免受涨价影响，但这种情况已经变得不可持续。库克没有透露苹果计划何时提高价格或哪些产品将受到影响。该公司已于 3 月份停止销售配备 512GB RAM 的 Mac Studio，随后在放弃了更便宜的 599 美元选项后，将 Mac Mini 的起价提高至 799 美元。分析师 Tim Culpan 还建议，苹果可能会停止 MacBook Neo 的基本配置，同时保留 699 美元、512GB 存储空间的型号。随着人工智能公司不断在其庞大的数据中心中需要更多的内存，供应商正在努力跟上。短缺导致 RAM 和存储成本飙升，以及游戏机、笔记本电脑和其他设备的价格上涨。库克对《华尔街日报》表示：“当消费者想要设备时，供应却减少了，而内存厂商却在大幅提价。” “我们绝对需要内存定价和供应才能恢复到消费产品的合理水平。”苹果正准备在今年晚些时候推出其最新的 iPhone 系列，不过目前尚不清楚内存短缺将对定价产生多大影响。 《华尔街日报》估计，即将推出的 iPhone 18 Pro 的售价可能会达到 1,299 美元，比 iPhone 17 Pro 的 1,099 美元有所上涨。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
